question: How many Korean restaurants have I tried in my city?

retrieval_results.context:
User has recently visited four Korean restaurants, each offering distinct flavors: one served an amazing bibimbap, another served a spicy kimchi stew, the third offered a unique Japchae made with sweet potato noodles, and the fourth provided a different culinary experience. The user is seeking new restaurant recommendations.
User is experimenting with different types of noodles and wants recommendations for popular Korean noodle dishes that can be made at home.
User is planning a dinner party featuring Korean-style BBQ beef as the main dish, with large‑quantity Korean BBQ, Indian dishes, and a Middle Eastern falafel that will be crispy on the outside and fluffy inside. The user also wants Indian‑inspired dishes that pair well with the Korean BBQ and falafel.
User is aware of a newly opened Indian restaurant in their neighborhood that serves chicken tikka masala, which they find appealing.
User chose kimchi and bokkeumbap as side dishes to accompany the Korean-style BBQ beef at the dinner party.
User is seeking recommendations for a high-quality basmati rice brand to use with saag aloo.
User selected samosas and saag aloo as Indian-inspired dishes for the dinner party.
User has been using an authentic Japanese short-grain rice brand and is uncertain if it is suitable for saag aloo, considering sticking with traditional Indian basmati rice.
User decided to use traditional Indian basmati rice for the dinner party.
